[[BLOCK:slide13_shape1]]
Proposed by Friedman (2008)  
Similar to abduction or IBE: avoid fixating on a single answer and consider multiple possibilities.  
There may be various solutions to a problem—why not explore different paths?  
Start with no modeling or zero coefficients.  
Experiment with a range of models. The solution is flexible (variable).  
Choose the optimal solution (determined by the algorithm, not manual selection)!
